{"id":"swarmstr-r7u.1","title":"TOOL-01a Introduce normalized tool descriptor types","description":"Recommendation anchor: TOOL-01a in docs/refactor/src-agent-platform-transfer-recommendations.md.

Create a normalized Go-native tool descriptor that unifies the metadata currently split across metiq's tool registry, profiles, and plugin manager.

Why this bead exists:
- In src, the Tool contract carries schema, execution traits, interrupt behavior, and runtime semantics in one place (`src/Tool.ts:158-469`).
- src can assemble built-in and MCP tools through one shared path because they share a canonical contract (`src/tools.ts:345-389`).
- metiq currently splits these concerns across `internal/agent/tools.go`, `internal/agent/profiles.go`, and `internal/plugins/manager/manager.go`, which makes later work on validation, scheduling, and unified assembly much harder.

Scope:
- Add a normalized descriptor type that can represent built-ins, plugin tools, and future remote tools.
- Include name, description, provider-visible schema/definition, origin/source, and execution traits.
- Keep this bead focused on descriptor design and integration points, not yet on changing runtime execution behavior.

Non-goals:
- Do not port src's ToolUseContext.
- Do not redesign ACP or context-engine interfaces here.","design":"Source anchors:
- src/Tool.ts:158-469
- src/tools.ts:345-389

Target touchpoints:

{"id":"swarmstr-r7u.4","title":"TOOL-02b Replace single middleware with phased execution hooks","description":"Recommendation anchor: TOOL-02b in docs/refactor/src-agent-platform-transfer-recommendations.md.

{"id":"swarmstr-2xq.2","title":"Make NIP-65 self-sync EOSE-aware and replaceable-event ordered","description":"Nostr audit finding NP-02.

Observed behavior:
- `internal/nostr/runtime/nip65.go:NIP65SelfSync()` tracks only `lastEventID`.
- Pre-EOSE events are not applied to runtime state; only post-EOSE updates trigger `OnRelayUpdate`.
- Live updates are accepted in arrival order instead of replaceable-event order.

Why this matters:
For replaceable metadata (`kind:10002`), the client should accumulate the best stored event during catch-up, mark backfill completion on EOSE, apply the authoritative startup state once, and then only accept newer live updates based on `(CreatedAt, EventID)` ordering.

Runtime risk:
The daemon can start with stale local relay state even when newer remote metadata already exists, and stale multi-relay deliveries can overwrite newer state after startup.

Expected direction:
Use an explicit catch-up/live boundary at EOSE and align ordering logic with the safer pattern already present in `relay_sets.go` and `capability.go`.
","acceptance_criteria":"- `NIP65SelfSync()` accumulates the best pre-EOSE event and applies it once after EOSE.
- Post-EOSE updates are accepted only when newer than current state by `(CreatedAt, EventID)`.
- Startup behavior does not silently keep stale local relays when authoritative remote metadata already exists.
- Tests cover pre-EOSE catch-up, post-EOSE live updates, and stale-vs-new ordering across multiple relays.

{"id":"swarmstr-2xq.4","title":"Check cached control responses before throttle and expiry in ControlRPCBus","description":"Nostr audit finding NP-04.

Observed behavior:
- `internal/nostr/runtime/control_bus.go:handleInbound()` marks the event seen, then applies caller throttling and request-age rejection before consulting cached replay for cacheable methods.

Why this matters:
Nostr delivery is at-least-once across relays/reconnects. Legitimate duplicate control requests should hit idempotent replay first, especially after relay delays, reconnects, or restarts.

Runtime risk:
A caller can resend a request that was already handled and receive `rate limited` or `expired` instead of the original result, weakening retry safety and replay semantics.

Expected direction:
Decode the call, normalize the method, and consult replay cache before applying freshness/throttle rejection for cacheable methods.

{"id":"swarmstr-3p3.1","title":"MCPCFG-01 Add an MCP config registry and resolution layer","description":"Recommendation anchor: MCPCFG-01 in docs/refactor/src-mcp-transfer-recommendations.md.

Replace swarmstr's current startup-only `extra.mcp` parsing with a real MCP config registry and resolution layer.

Why this bead exists:
- `src` treats MCP configuration as a resolved inventory, not a single raw map. It supports scoped config resolution, precedence, deduplication, and policy/trust filtering before connection (`src/services/mcp/config.ts:619-759`, `src/services/mcp/config.ts:1033-1252`).
- swarmstr currently parses one `extra.mcp` block in `internal/mcp/manager.go` and wires it directly into startup registration in `cmd/metiqd/main.go:1001-1085`.
- Without a resolved config inventory, swarmstr cannot cleanly add reconnect, trust policy, CLI management, dynamic sources, or project-vs-managed MCP behavior.

Scope:
- Introduce a resolved MCP server inventory API that can aggregate configured sources and expose stable server metadata to the runtime.
- Define precedence/merge rules appropriate for swarmstr's control/config architecture.
- Add dedup hooks so future dynamic/plugin/project sources do not spawn duplicate MCP connections.

Non-goals:
- Do not implement OAuth flows in this bead.
- Do not add generic resources/prompts surfaces yet.
","design":"Primary references:
- docs/refactor/src-mcp-transfer-recommendations.md
- src/services/mcp/types.ts:10-26
- src/services/mcp/config.ts:619-759,1033-1252

Target touchpoints:
- swarmstr/internal/mcp/manager.go
- swarmstr/internal/gateway/methods/config_mutation.go
- swarmstr/cmd/metiqd/main.go
","acceptance_criteria":"- swarmstr has a canonical MCP config resolution path instead of reading `extra.mcp` directly at the connection site.

- `src` treats MCP configuration as a resolved inventory, not a single raw map. It supports scoped config resolution, precedence, deduplication, and policy/trust filtering before connection (`src/services/mcp/config.ts:619-759`, `src/services/mcp/config.ts:1033-1252`).
- swarmstr currently parses one `extra.mcp` block in `internal/mcp/manager.go` and wires it directly into startup registration in `cmd/metiqd/main.go:1001-1085`.
- Without a resolved config inventory, swarmstr cannot cleanly add reconnect, trust policy, CLI management, dynamic sources, or project-vs-managed MCP behavior.

Scope:
- Introduce a resolved MCP server inventory API that can aggregate configured sources and expose stable server metadata to the runtime.
- Define precedence/merge rules appropriate for swarmstr's control/config architecture.
- Add dedup hooks so future dynamic/plugin/project sources do not spawn duplicate MCP connections.

Non-goals:
- Do not implement OAuth flows in this bead.
- Do not add generic resources/prompts surfaces yet.

Upgrade swarmstr's external MCP manager from a startup-time connector into a stateful connection manager with explicit per-server states, reconnect behavior, and capability refresh.

Why this bead exists:
- `src` models MCP servers as connected, failed, needs-auth, pending, or disabled and provides reconnect helpers plus capability-aware refresh behavior (`src/services/mcp/types.ts:180-226`, `src/services/mcp/client.ts:595-764`, `src/services/mcp/client.ts:2130-2249`).
- swarmstr currently either connects a server during startup or logs the failure; it does not maintain a richer lifecycle model in `internal/mcp/manager.go`.
- Immediate reconnection/capability refresh support is required before auth, resource/prompt discovery, or operator-facing health management can feel reliable.

Scope:
- Add a per-server state model to the manager.
- Support explicit reconnect and post-reconnect capability refresh.
- Separate discovery state from one-time startup success/failure.

Non-goals:
- Do not implement OAuth token acquisition here.
- Do not build CLI commands yet.
